resposta:
solution: ```class Solution:
    def minMovesToMakePalindrome(self, s: str) -> int:
        s = list(s)
        n = len(s)
        res = 0
        left, right = 0, n - 1
        while left < right:
            if s[left] == s[right]:
                left += 1
                right -= 1
                continue
            k = right
            while k > left and s[k] != s[left]:
                k -= 1
            if k == left:
                s[left], s[left + 1] = s[left + 1], s[left]
                res += 1
                continue
            for i in range(k, right):
                s[i], s[i + 1] = s[i + 1], s[i]
                res += 1
            left += 1
            right -= 1
        return res
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Greedy two-pointer sweep: fix the outermost pair by bringing the matching character to its mirror position via adjacent swaps, counting each swap. When the left character has no twin except itself (odd count), swap it once to move it toward the center and continue. This minimizes total swaps by always fixing the outermost pair first.

class Solution:
    def minMovesToMakePalindrome(self, s: str) -> int:
        s = list(s)
        n = len(s)
        res = 0
        left, right = 0, n - 1
        while left < right:
            if s[left] == s[right]:
                left += 1
                right -= 1
                continue
            k = right
            while k > left and s[k] != s[left]:
                k -= 1
            if k == left:
                s[left], s[left + 1] = s[left + 1], s[left]
                res += 1
                continue
            for i in range(k, right):
                s[i], s[i + 1] = s[i + 1], s[i]
                res += 1
            left += 1
            right -= 1
        return res
